data.title: Macy's outlines raised full-year EPS guidance to $2–$2.20 amid strong comp sales and operational gains
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Macy's, Inc. (M) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Antony Spring, CEO & Chairman, stated that Macy's is "encouraged by recent results, which reflect the accelerating momentum of our Bold New Chapter strategy." He highlighted that all key metrics—net sales, comparable sales, core adjusted EBITDA, and adjusted diluted EPS—"exceeded our guidance with positive contributions from each nameplate." Macy's achieved its strongest comparable growth in 13 quarters, led by the Go-Forward business, which delivered another quarter of positive comps. Bloomingdale's posted its fifth consecutive quarter of growth and its best comp in 13 quarters, while Bluemercury delivered another quarter of comparable sales growth.
  • Spring reported, "Macy's, Inc. delivered a positive 3.2% comp with Go-Forward Macy's, Inc. continuing to outperform, growing 3.4%. Adjusted EPS of $0.09 was well above our guidance range of a loss of $0.15 to $0.20 and above last year's third quarter result of $0.04."
  • Spring emphasized customer experience improvements, noting, "Macy's delivered its highest third quarter Net Promoter Score on record." He also described the launch of new brands and expanded partnerships, citing introductions such as Rodd & Gunn, Reiss, and Prada Beauty, and expansions of Barbour, Mackenzie-Childs, and MFK.
  • Spring discussed the impact of store initiatives, stating the Reimagine 125 stores were up 2.7%, with fine jewelry, watches, handbags, men's career, and ready-to-wear outperforming the total Macy's comp.
  • In luxury, Spring said, "Bloomingdale's delivered another impressive quarter, achieving a positive 9% comp, which was its best in 13 quarters as well as a sequential improvement in its Net Promoter Score."
  • Thomas Edwards, COO & CFO, stated, "Macy's, Inc. comparable sales of 3.2% were our strongest in 13 quarters. Go-forward comps were up 3.4%, with growth across all nameplates. An adjusted EPS of $0.09 was well above the high end of our guidance on better-than-expected sales, gross margin and SG&A."

Outlook

  • Edwards provided fourth quarter guidance, stating, "For the fourth quarter, we expect net sales of approximately $7.35 billion to $7.5 billion... comparable sales to be down approximately 2.5% to flat with go-forward comparable sales down 2% to flat." He added, "Core adjusted EBITDA as a percent of total revenue of 9.4% to 10.1% and adjusted EPS of $1.35 to $1.55."
  • Edwards outlined full year 2025 guidance: "net sales of approximately $21.475 billion to $21.625 billion... comparable sales to be flat to up 0.5%, with Macy's, Inc. Go-Forward comparable sales to be flat to up roughly 1%." He raised expected adjusted EPS to $2 to $2.20.
  • The company noted asset sale gains are now expected to be $60 million to $65 million, below prior expectations.

Financial Results

  • Macy's, Inc. net sales were $4.7 billion, down 0.6% or about $29 million from last year. Excluding the impact of planned closures, sales grew 2.9%. Total revenue was $4.9 billion.
  • Macy's net sales were down 2.3%, while comparable sales were up 2%; Go-Forward comps rose 2.3%. Reimagine 125 comparable sales rose 2.7%.
  • Bloomingdale's net sales rose 8.6% and comparable sales were up 9%. Bluemercury net sales were up 3.8% and comparable sales were up 1.1%.
  • Gross margin was $1.9 billion or 39.4% of net sales. SG&A expense was $2 billion, down $40 million from last year. Core adjusted EBITDA was $273 million or 5.6% of total revenue.
  • Inventory dollars rose 0.7% versus last year, in line with expectations. Operating cash flow year-to-date was $247 million, with free cash flow an outflow of $183 million. Capital expenditures were $525 million. The company ended the quarter with $447 million in cash.

Q&A

  • Matthew Boss, JPMorgan: Asked about traction in reimagined store initiatives and comp trends. Spring responded, "We are definitely seeing traction in the R 125... That's positive growth in both the first 50 and the next 75, that sequential improvement, a 2.7% comp."
  • Brooke Roach, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about sustaining momentum and EBITDA margin delivery amid tariffs. Spring said, "The drivers of the business are... having the right product assortment... a good physical business and a good digital business." Edwards added, "We had been looking at a 40 to 60 basis point impact. It's now 40 to 50... due to... mitigation efforts."
  • Blake Anderson, Jefferies: Asked about aspirational customers and SG&A savings. Spring explained, "The aspirational customer is a good thing... our job is to make sure that the breadth of our assortments, value, promotions, price points cater to that customer."
  • Charles Grom, Gordon Haskett: Pressed on confidence in the consumer and Q4 guidance. Spring stated, "I'm more confident today in our strategy than I was 90 days ago... Our guidance for the fourth quarter is actually higher than our previous implied guide."
  • Alexandra Straton, Morgan Stanley: Questioned competitive landscape and gross margin pressure. Spring responded, "We are better than a year ago... we are very well positioned for the holidays." Edwards noted, "In Q4... 70 to 100 basis points is due to tariffs."
  • Additional questions covered pricing, credit business, store closures, digital/store mix, category performance, and newness in assortments.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was slightly positive, with a focus on strategic progress, competitive positioning, and sustainability of recent gains. Several congratulated management on the quarter and pressed for details on guidance and margin headwinds.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, often emphasizing confidence in strategy and operational progress. Spring stated, "I have every confidence in the team... We are well positioned to deliver the quarter." Edwards consistently highlighted disciplined expense management and mitigation of external pressures.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more assertive about strategy execution, while analysts maintained cautious optimism, probing for risks and sustainability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year adjusted EPS was raised to $2 to $2.20, up from the previous quarter’s range of $1.70 to $2.05.
  • Third quarter comparable sales growth accelerated to 3.2% from 1.9% in the previous quarter, with Go-Forward comp growth also improving.
  • Bloomingdale's comp growth increased to 9% from 5.7%, and Bluemercury remained positive.
  • Gross margin rate slightly decreased versus last year but improved versus internal expectations due to tariff mitigation.
  • Management sentiment was more positive and confident, while analysts sustained a similar level of cautious optimism.
  • Strategic focus remained on store enhancements, digital growth, and cost controls, but with increased emphasis on luxury and operational simplification.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariffs continue to impact gross margin, though management reported successful mitigation through cost negotiations, vendor discounts, and pricing adjustments.
  • Store closures present both a risk and an opportunity, with ongoing plans for further optimization and recapture strategies for affected customers.
  • The consumer environment, described as "choiceful," introduces uncertainty, especially in the holiday season, though management is maintaining inventory and promotional flexibility.
  • Asset sale gains are expected to be lower than previous guidance, impacting EPS.

Final Takeaway

Macy's, Inc. management highlighted another quarter of broad-based operational and financial improvements, with strong momentum across Macy's, Bloomingdale's, and Bluemercury. The company delivered better-than-expected results, raised full-year EPS guidance, and reinforced confidence in the Bold New Chapter strategy, while remaining proactive in mitigating tariff and consumer headwinds. Management expressed optimism about holiday positioning and ongoing initiatives to drive sustainable, profitable growth across all nameplates.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: 3M taps former Zeldin aide as public affairs role gains CEO oversight
data.description:
3M (MMM) elevated Kevin Dowling, its head of U.S. government affairs, to report directly to CEO Bill Brown, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing people familiar with the change. The move comes as the company continues to face regulatory and legal fallout tied to its historic production of PFAS, which are known as “forever chemicals.”

Dowling previously served as a senior aide steering policy for Lee Zeldin during his time in Congress and later worked at Venn Strategies before joining 3M (MMM) in 2024.

The shift in 3M’s lobbying and public-policy operation follows intensified scrutiny of PFAS, chemicals linked to cancer, fertility issues and immune-system effects. The Biden administration had set strict drinking-water limits, but the EPA under President Trump has delayed compliance deadlines, withdrawn some standards and asked a court to void others, citing flaws in how they were crafted. At the same time, the agency has kept certain PFAS classified as hazardous under Superfund law, a designation business groups say could saddle companies with tens of billions in cleanup costs.

The EPA is also reconsidering a rule that would have required PFAS manufacturers to file a decade’s worth of environmental and health data, proposing a scaled-back reporting requirement instead.

data.title: U.S. stocks rise as investors shrug off lower-than-expected jobs data, struggling tech stocks
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Wednesday as the Nasdaq Composite ((COMP:IND)) inched up to recover after the latest private sector jobs data and Microsoft's decline dragged sentiment.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.4% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.9%.

Microsoft (MSFT) shares fell after opening as The Information reported that the tech giant is cutting software sales quotas tied to artificial intelligence. The company -- currently -1.6% -- later refuted the report. 

The U.S. private sector employment fell by 32K in November, compared with the 5K consensus and October's 47K (revised from 42K), according to data released by ADP on Wednesday.

“It’s a K-shaped economy and labor market as the Nov ADP private payroll estimate indicated a 32K decline in hiring. For the past 6 out of 7 months small businesses have seen losses and were down -120K. These are firms without adequate capital depth and are likely experiencing stress in adjusting to higher prices and taxes such as tariffs,” said Joseph Brusuelas, RSM U.S. principal and chief economist.

“However, mid-size businesses added 51K and large firms added 39K as those businesses have exposure to the upper spur of the K that is doing quite well right now,” Brusuelas added.

Also, the S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI came in softer than expected at 54.2 in November, compared to October's 54.6, while the U.S. ISM Services Index edged up in November, beating consensus.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) continued its recovery, now over $92,800.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.06%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 3.49%.

On the earnings side, Salesforce (CRM) and Snowflake (SNOW), among others, will report their results.

In other economic news, the U.S. import and export prices were unchanged month-over-month in September.

data.title: Congressman Marjorie Taylor Greene discloses Bitcoin ETF purchase
data.description:
• Congressman Marjorie Taylor Greene disclosed that she has bought up to $15,000 worth of the iShares Bitcoin ETF (IBIT) on Wednesday.
  • Congressman Greene, who last month announced she would be resigning from Congress, made the purchase on Nov. 21, according to a financial disclosure form. 
  • In recent days, the 50-year-old Congresswoman representing the 14th district in Georgia also disclosed purchases of U.S. Treasury Bills worth between $50,001 and $100,000; buying between $15,001 and $50,000 worth of ADP (ADP) and Paychex (PAYX) shares; and between $15,0001 and $50,000 worth of Procter & Gamble (PG).
  • Greene, who said her last day in Congress will be Jan. 5, 2026, has been one of the more active legislators in terms of purchasing stocks. In 2025, she has filed 24 Congressional trading forms, including one a week after President Trump's inauguration.
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Kinetik started with Buy rating at Jefferies, expecting company to reach potential
data.description:
Kinetik Holdings (KNTK) +3.7% in Wednesday's trading as Jefferies initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $41 price target, citing growth prospects that have been overly discounted after a disappointing FY 2025 partially attributable to macro headwinds, particularly Waha Hub discounts.

Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith said he thinks the headwinds Kinetik (KNTK) faced in 2025 through weak Waha pricing will be mitigated by the impending wave of Permian Basin egress additions; with ~9 billion cf/day of Permian residue pipeline capacity coming online through year-end 2028, the analyst expects Waha pricing will be less of an overhang in the near-
term, benefiting the company in the form of higher margins and less restricted volume flow from producers.

Kinetik (KNTK) could see $120 million-$180 million improvement to adjusted EBITDA as it reprices above-market natural gas liquids contracts signed in the mid-2010s in a more competitive, lower cost environment. Dumoulin-Smith said, adding that he is constructive on the company's ability to grow without spending material capital spending, forecasting $460 million growth in adjusted EBITDA through FY 2030 on just $1. billion in total capex.

The analyst also sees Kinetik (KNTK) as one of the more compelling M&A targets in the midstream, as its presence in the Northern Delaware and magnitude of uncommitted equity NGL volumes by ~2029 could offer compelling synergies to a potential acquirer, which should be better reflected in the stock's valuation.

data.title: European defense chiefs are said to plan December talks on next-gen fighter
data.description:
European defense officials from Germany, France and Spain are expected to gather on December 11 for high-level talks on the Future Combat Air System (FCAS), Europe’s planned next-generation fighter program, according to media reports.

FCAS, an ambitious €100 billion ($117 billion) effort launched more than eight years ago, has struggled to move forward due to ongoing disputes among its industrial partners over who gets which portion of the work and who controls key technologies.

News of the upcoming meeting was first reported by Bloomberg.

The countries are trying to unlock the next development stage of a system that envisions a new fighter aircraft operating alongside unmanned drones by around 2040, a concept broadly similar to the collaborative GCAP project underway between the United Kingdom, Italy and Japan.

But negotiations have repeatedly stalled, largely because of friction between Dassault Aviation (OTCPK:DUAVF), which leads the effort on the French side, and Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY), which represents German and Spanish industrial interests.

French President Emmanuel Macron and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz met last month to attempt to break the deadlock and reinvigorate the program, but no major breakthrough has yet been announced.

data.title: Nvidia in focus as company touts NVL72 performance on top AI models
data.description:
• Nvidia (NVDA) said on Wednesday that its Blackwell NVL72 rack system is capable of running the latest artificial intelligence models and improving their performance by up to 10 times.
  • Models such as Kimi's K2 Thinking, DeepSeek-R1, Mistral Large 3 and others are capable of running 10 times faster on the GB200 NVL72 system, when compared to Nvidia's HGX H200 system, Nvidia said in a blog post. All of these models have a mixture-of-experts, or MoE, architecture that “mimics the efficiency of the human brain,” Nvidia added.
  • Kimi's K2 Thinking and DeepSeek's (DEEPSEEK) R1 model are two of the most popular models in China. Mistral announced its Large 3 model earlier this week. 
  • The GB200 NVL72 system incorporates 72 of Nvidia's Grace Blackwell GPUs into a single system and incorporates links between them to improve performance. The GB200 NVL72 system went into mass production in October.
  • Separately, on Wednesday, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said he just met with President Trump to discuss export controls amid some concern from lawmakers on both sides of the aisle that advanced AI chips could go to China.
  • Nvidia shares fell fractionally in midday trading.

data.title: Lockheed Martin opens hypersonics lab in Alabama as U.S. races global rivals
data.description:
Lockheed Martin (LMT) opened a new facility in Alabama dedicated to integrating hypersonic weapons, marking another step in the United States’ effort to accelerate development of the cutting-edge technology amid rapid advances by China and Russia.

The defense contractor unveiled a 17,000-square-foot system integration laboratory in Huntsville, part of roughly $700 million in recent and planned investments by its strategic and missile defense division to expand its hypersonics footprint.

 

 

U.S. defense officials have been working to build out an industrial base capable of producing hypersonic systems at scale. That push has taken on greater urgency as Russia has already used hypersonic weapons in Ukraine, underscoring the time pressure facing the Pentagon.

Global competition intensifies

Washington is pursuing both offensive and defensive hypersonic capabilities while Moscow and Beijing continue to conduct frequent tests and move toward fielding operational systems. Hypersonic weapons can travel at speeds above Mach 5 and maneuver unpredictably, complicating detection and interception.

Lockheed Martin (LMT) has become one of the Pentagon’s key partners in developing the technology, securing multi-billion-dollar contracts to build separate hypersonic systems for the Air Force and Navy. The company is also responsible for integrating the Army’s Long-Range Hypersonic Weapon onto a mobile launch platform. Alongside Leidos Dynetics (LDOS), it is producing the Common Hypersonic Glide Body, the core component for Army and Navy missiles.

The Army expects to take delivery of its first battery of hypersonic missiles later this month after a two-year pause related to testing challenges. A major flight test is planned once the service receives the new weapons. Although the delay is notable, Army officials say the program remains on an accelerated schedule compared with typical missile development timelines, which often stretch beyond a decade.

Supporting future production

Lockheed (LMT) said the Huntsville lab will play an important role as the Pentagon shifts from prototyping and testing to establishing a steady production rhythm for hypersonic systems. The facility is intended to support the broader infrastructure needed to mature the technology and bring multiple programs into sustained manufacturing.

data.title: Fanatics rolls out prediction markets in 24 states, including California, Texas, and Florida
data.description:
Fanatics (FANA) announced the launch of a prediction market platform built to let people trade on the moments shaping sports, finance, and culture. The company said Fanatics Markets will introduce customers to markets and pricing offered by Crypto.com | Derivatives North America, a CFTC-registered exchange and clearinghouse. The CFTC-registered derivatives exchange is anticipated to provide institutional-grade infrastructure for Fanatics Markets to deliver a best-in-class customer experience. 

Phase One for the platform is already live, with event contracts for sports, finance, economics, and politics. Phase Two, which is launching early next year, will see Fanatics Markets expand into event contracts related to crypto, stocks/IPOs, climate, pop culture, tech/AI, movies, and music.  

Fanatics Markets is launching in 24 states, including states such as California, Texas, Florida, and Washington that do not have legalized widespread sports betting.

"For years, Fanatics has given fans new ways to enhance their fandom through team merchandise, collectibles, tickets, gaming, events, and more," said Matt King, Chief Executive Officer, Fanatics Betting and Gaming. "Now, with Fanatics Markets, we’re giving fans a safe and intuitive way to engage with the moments that move sports and culture and to pick a side and profit along the way if their prediction is correct," he added.

Fanatics (FANA) is entering a fast-growing prediction market industry in the U.S. Currently, the main prediction market platforms are Kalshi (KALSHI), Robinhood (HOOD), Crypto.com (via Nadex), Polymarket (POLYMARKET), Interactive Brokers' (IBKR) ForecastTrader, and PredictIt. Meanwhile, sports betting giant DraftKings (DKNG) entered the prediction market space by acquiring CFTC-regulated Railbird to launch DraftKings Predictions, a mobile app expected soon targeting non-sportsbook states like California and Texas with event contracts on finance, culture, entertainment, and sports outcomes, while FanDuel (FLUT) partnered with CME Group to operate via a Futures Commission Merchant model for its own prediction market.

data.title: Vertex upgraded at Morgan Stanley on kidney pipeline
data.description:
• Morgan Stanley upgraded Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) to overweight from equal-weight citing optimism over several candidates in the pipeline to treat kidney conditions.
  • The bank boosted its price target to $516 from $438 (~19% upside based on Dec. 2 close).
  • Analyst Terence Flynn highlighted povetacicept and inaxaplin, both in phase 3 for, respectively, IgA nephropathy and APOL1-mediated kidney disease. Phase 3 data is expected in 2026, and if successful, "these drugs will continue VRTX's diversification away from its entrenched cystic fibrosis franchise (98% in 2025 vs. 49% in 2035) and accelerate the company's revenue/EPS growth."
  • He sees the probability of success for povetacicept of 75% and 50% for inaxaplin, though he added that inaxaplin could represent a huge opportunity given there are no approved treatments for APOL1-mediated kidney disease. 
  • For povetacicept, Flynn projects risk-adjusted sales in 2035 of $3.5B -- assuming additional indications -- and $2.6B for inaxaplin.
